evals.prompt: Add multi-language support to my Next.js App Router project. I need English and French.
evals.expected_output: Install next-intl. Create messages/en.json and messages/fr.json. Add locale detection in middleware.ts using Accept-Language header. Create app/[lang]/layout.tsx with locale param. Load translations server-side via getMessages(). Add hreflang tags in generateMetadata. Pre-render both locales with generateStaticParams.

evals.prompt: I have hardcoded English strings scattered throughout my JSX. My designer wants to add Spanish.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No hardcoded strings in JSX. Replace all raw text with translation keys: {t('nav.home')} instead of 'Home'. Load translation dictionaries server-side with getMessages() to keep client bundle small. Create messages/es.json with all keys. Use next-intl's useTranslations hook in Client Components only.

evals.prompt: My i18n URLs use both /en/about and /about/en depending on the page. Users complain they can't share links.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No mixed URL locale patterns. Choose one pattern and use it consistently. Recommendation: sub-paths (/en/about, /fr/about) — best for SEO and caching. Configure in next.config.js i18n settings. Update middleware.ts to enforce the pattern. Add canonical/hreflang tags for all translated routes.

should_not_trigger: Write a Go REST API for user management
Set up a Redis cache with expiration
